hints.et and tu: 'Et' is a conjunction and 'tu' is a pronoun — 'Vade' and 'fac' are the verbs
hints.tu and similiter: 'Tu' is a pronoun and 'similiter' is an adverb — 'Vade' and 'fac' are verbs
hints.Vade only: 'Vade' is one verb, but 'fac' is also an imperative verb meaning 'do/make'

hints.to wade: English 'wade' is unrelated — 'Vade' comes from 'vadere' (to go)
hints.to be strong: That would be 'valere' — 'Vade' comes from 'vadere' (to go)
hints.to invade: That would be 'invadere' — 'Vade' comes from 'vadere' (to go)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'vadit' (3rd pers.) — 'Vade' is imperative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'vadat' — 'Vade' is imperative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be a past tense form — 'Vade' is a present command

hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — a synonym but different verb
hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee)

hints.to speak: That would be 'dicere/loqui' — 'fac' comes from 'facere' (to do/make)
hints.to carry: That would be 'ferre' — 'fac' comes from 'facere' (to do/make)
hints.to sacrifice: That would be 'sacrificare' — 'fac' comes from 'facere' (to do/make)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'facit' — 'fac' is the irregular imperative of 'facere'
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'faciat' — 'fac' is imperative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fecit' — 'fac' is present imperative

hints.ago, agere, egi, actum: These are the parts of 'agere' (to drive/do)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the parts of 'dare' (to give)
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the parts of 'ferre' (to carry/bear)

steps.prompt: 'Fac' is an irregular imperative. The regular form would be?

hints.faci: 3rd conj. imperatives drop the -ere ending — the expected form is 'face', but 'fac' is the usual form
hints.faca: This is not a valid Latin form — the regular form would be 'face'
hints.facie: This is not a valid imperative form — the regular form would be 'face'

hints.adjective: An adjective would modify a noun — 'similiter' modifies the verb 'fac'
hints.conjunction: A conjunction would join clauses — 'similiter' modifies how one should act
hints.preposition: A preposition would govern a noun — 'similiter' is an adverb
